title: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme
text: <
crumbs: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme

title: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme
text:
About
The Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DS) is a tiered, in‑person training pathway at the University of Nottingham designed to build core digital competencies, data literacy, and research software skills for modern, reproducible, data-intensive research. It supports doctoral researchers in progressing from foundational fluency to domain-specific expertise.
<

Programme MaterialsStructureDelivery


Essential Digital Skills (EDS) is the foundation tier of DS&DS. It establishes baseline competence in:

Computational Environment & Tools
Reproducible Workflows
Research Data Management
Collaboration & Open Scholarship

👉 Access EDS programme



DS&DS spans three tiers, aligned with institutional priorities in open science and research integrity:

Foundation – Essential Digital Skills (Year 1, mandatory)
Intermediate – Methods for Data Science (Year 2+, optional)
Advanced – Domain-Specific Specialisations (tailored)

This progression builds confidence and research-ready capability at every stage.



The programme is modular and primarily in-person:

Year 1: Core skills workshops (EDS)
Year 2: Optional intermediate/advanced modules
Cohort delivery initially for the BBSRC DTP (2025)

All materials are developed for reuse and open dissemination.
crumbs: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme

title: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme
text:
Curriculum & Outcomes
Participants will:

Manage data using FAIR principles
Build reproducible pipelines (Git, Quarto, Jupyter)
Analyse data with statistical and domain-relevant tools
Document and share research outputs openly
Operate within governance and policy frameworks

All materials are shared openly via GitHub, with linked workshops and exercises.
crumbs: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme

title: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme
text:
Participants

Open to BBSRC doctoral researchers at Nottingham
Designed for those with some / no prior coding experience
Delivered in inclusive, discipline-agnostic format
Encourages peer-led learning and shared practice

Progression is supported via optional pathways following completion of the foundation modules.
crumbs: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme

title: Data Science and Digital Skills Programme
text:
Contact

Programme Lead: Dr. Thomas Giles

Email: tom.giles@nottingham.ac.uk

For registration, accessibility, or module questions, please contact the DS&DS team directly.

title: Essential Digital Skill:
text:
This course establishes the baseline digital literacy and governance awareness required for reproducible, collaborative research at Nottingham. This component will give early career researchers practical familiarity with institutional systems, data policies, management good practice and introduces the basic concepts of agile delivery.

Talk 1:

Web View
Slides

Talk 2:

Web View
Slidxes

[Talk 3]
[Talk 4]
[Talk 5]
[Talk 6]
[Talk 7]
[Talk 8]



EDS is the foundation tier of DS&DSP. This in-person training programme is designed to equip researchers with the fundamental digital competencies, institutional knowledge, and research practices required to operate effectively in the University of Nottingham’s modern, data-intensive research environment.







The course comprises 12 thematic modules delivered over several in-person training days, with each module structured to introduce key concepts and tools, followed by exercises and peer-led activities that consolidate learning in a practical, research-specific context.



Modules will be delivered through a blended format that combines short (10–15-minute) teaching segments with interactive activities such as case tasks, scenario-based discussions, matching exercises, and prompt testing. Group-based engagement will be used to encourage collaboration, reflection, and shared problem-solving, with a consistent research-focused framing to ensure relevance across disciplines.



There is no formal summative assessment for the EDS programme. Participants will be encouraged to complete self-directed learning logs after each training day, where they summarise key insights, identify tools or practices to explore further, and reflect on how the learning applies to their own research. These reflective materials are intended to support personal development planning and may inform supervisory discussions or future training reviews.



To support iterative improvement and ensure alignment with researcher needs, feedback will be gathered through structured post-session surveys (anonymous and standardised), optional open comments on specific sessions, tools, or teaching methods, and facilitated group reflection or informal check-ins at the end of each day. This feedback will inform the ongoing refinement of content, delivery, and alignment with institutional tools and policy frameworks.


By the end of the EDS, participants will have established a baseline in digital fluency, reproducibility, and policy compliance. They will gain a working understanding of the University’s research platforms, infrastructure, and governance frameworks, and will be well prepared to progress into more technical, hands-on, or domain-specialised training.
